steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The sentence has two parallel independent clauses joined by "and." The first clause has subject "DL" (singular) with verb "pertains." The second clause has subject "TML" (singular). The blank must be a verb that matches "TML."

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and parallel structure — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
The verb must be singular to match "TML" and parallel in form to "pertains" in the first clause.
  • Choice A — are pertaining plural present progressive. "Are" is plural and does not agree with the singular "TML." Eliminated.
  • Choice B — pertains singular simple present. Agrees with singular "TML" and is parallel to "pertains" in the first clause. Correct.
  • Choice C — have pertained plural present perfect. "Have" is plural and breaks agreement with "TML." Eliminated.
  • Choice D — pertain base form / plural present. Appropriate for plural subjects, not singular "TML." Eliminated.
